skill.name: performance-profiling
skill.description: Performance profiling principles. Measurement, analysis, and optimization techniques.
skill.summary: Measure, analyze, and optimize web application performance using Lighthouse and browser developer tools.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Evaluated 11 static security findings. All patterns detected are false positives. The subprocess.run call uses list arguments (not shell=True), preventing command injection. Temp file operations use the safe tempfile module. Documentation examples in SKILL.md are not executable code. The 'cryptographic algorithm' alerts were misidentified keywords. This is a legitimate performance profiling skill with no security concerns.

high_findings.title: Subprocess Execution
high_findings.description: subprocess.run called at line 22 to execute lighthouse CLI. Arguments passed as list (not shell=True), preventing command injection. User input (URL) is safely passed as direct argument.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: subprocess.run with list arguments is safe from shell injection. No user-controlled command concatenation occurs.

high_findings.title: Cryptographic Algorithm Detection
high_findings.description: Scanner flagged 'measure, analyze, optimize' keywords as cryptographic algorithms. This is a false positive from keyword pattern matching.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Keywords 'measure', 'analyze', 'optimize' are performance terminology, not cryptographic algorithms.

medium_findings.title: File System Operations
medium_findings.description: Temp file creation and cleanup using os module. Legitimate use for storing Lighthouse report output.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Uses Python's tempfile module for safe temporary file handling with proper cleanup.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Shell Examples
medium_findings.description: Backtick syntax in documentation showing example commands. These are documentation, not executable code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md contains documentation and example syntax, not actual executable shell commands.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in Documentation
low_findings.description: URL string appears in docstring as usage example. Not actual network code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: This is a docstring example, not network request code.

content.value_statement: This skill helps developers measure and optimize web application performance using Lighthouse audits and browser developer tools. It provides a structured workflow for identifying bottlenecks and improving Core Web Vitals scores.
actual_capabilities: Run Lighthouse performance audits via CLI script
Analyze Core Web Vitals metrics (LCP, INP, CLS)
Identify bundle size issues and optimization opportunities
Apply runtime profiling techniques using DevTools
Prioritize performance fixes for maximum impact
limitations: Requires Lighthouse CLI to be installed separately
Cannot profile local development servers without running them
Performance results vary based on network conditions
Does not provide real-time monitoring in production

use_cases.title: Frontend Developer Optimizing Load Speed
use_cases.description: A frontend developer wants to improve their web app's loading performance. They use the skill to run Lighthouse audits, identify the biggest performance bottlenecks, and apply targeted optimizations.

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Setting Up CI Performance Checks
use_cases.description: A DevOps engineer wants to add automated performance testing to their CI/CD pipeline. They use the skill's Lighthouse script to run audits on each deployment and fail builds that fall below performance thresholds.

use_cases.title: Full-Stack Developer Troubleshooting Runtime Issues
use_cases.description: A full-stack developer notices slow interactions on their web app. They use the skill to profile runtime performance, identify long tasks, and find memory leaks using DevTools Memory tab.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Lighthouse Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a Lighthouse performance audit on https://example.com using the performance-profiling skill. Show me the performance score and main recommendations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick performance check on a live website

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Core Web Vitals
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the performance-profiling skill, explain what Core Web Vitals (LCP, INP, CLS) measure and provide tips for improving each metric.

prompt_templates.title: Bundle Optimization Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the performance-profiling skill to analyze a large JavaScript bundle. What common issues should I look for and what are the recommended optimization actions?

prompt_templates.title: Performance Workflow Implementation
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the 4-step profiling workflow from the performance-profiling skill (BASELINE, IDENTIFY, FIX, VALIDATE), help me optimize a slow web application. Start with establishing a baseline.

output_examples.input: Run Lighthouse audit on my website
output_examples.output:
Performance Score: 78/100

Key Findings:
- LCP: 2.8s (Needs improvement)
- CLS: 0.05 (Good)
- INP: 180ms (Good)

Recommendations:
1. Eliminate render-blocking resources
2. Properly size images
3. Reduce JavaScript execution time

output_examples.input: What are the Core Web Vitals thresholds?
output_examples.output:
Core Web Vitals Thresholds:

| Metric | Good | Poor |
|-------|------|------|
| LCP | < 2.5s | > 4.0s |
| INP | < 200ms | > 500ms |
| CLS | < 0.1 | > 0.25 |

best_practices: Always establish a performance baseline before making changes
Focus on the biggest bottleneck first rather than micro-optimizations
Validate improvements with repeated measurements to account for variance
anti_patterns: Guessing at performance problems without profiling first
Optimizing code before identifying the actual bottleneck
Ignoring real user data in favor of synthetic testing only

faq.question: What tools does this skill use?
faq.answer: This skill uses Lighthouse for performance auditing and browser DevTools for runtime profiling. Lighthouse CLI must be installed separately.

faq.question: Do I need to install any dependencies?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to install Lighthouse CLI globally with: npm install -g lighthouse

faq.question: Can this skill profile local websites?
faq.answer: Yes, you can run Lighthouse against localhost or any locally running development server.

faq.question: What are Core Web Vitals?
faq.answer: Core Web Vitals are Google's standardized metrics: Largest Contentful Paint (LCP), Interaction to Next Paint (INP), and Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS).

faq.question: How often should I run performance audits?
faq.answer: Run audits during development, before deployments, and regularly in production using Real User Monitoring (RUM).

faq.question: What if my performance score is poor?
faq.answer: Use the 4-step workflow: establish a baseline, identify the biggest bottleneck, make one targeted fix, then validate the improvement before moving to the next issue.
